references.name: NHS accessibility guidance
references.borrowed: Kept accessibility as a whole-team constraint and aligned the stage with WCAG 2.2-aware semantics.
references.rejected: Did not introduce patient-facing NHS component styling into the staff workstation shell.

references.name: NHS content guide
references.borrowed: Used direct labels and short status lines for trust, provenance, and actionability.
references.rejected: Did not add explanatory help text or tutorial copy to the live shell.

references.name: WAI-ARIA APG complementary landmark
references.borrowed: Promoted, pinned, downgraded, and folded stages render as uniquely labelled complementary regions.
references.rejected: Did not add multiple unlabelled complementary landmarks.

references.name: WAI-ARIA APG tabs
references.borrowed: The narrow folded state uses tablist, tab, aria-selected, aria-controls, and tabpanel semantics.
references.rejected: Did not use tabs as navigation or as a separate mobile information architecture.

references.name: WAI-ARIA APG disclosure
references.borrowed: Promote and collapse controls use native buttons with aria-expanded and aria-controls.
references.rejected: Did not hide trust or provenance detail behind mandatory disclosure.

references.name: WAI-ARIA APG modal dialog
references.borrowed: Used modal guidance as a negative reference: the assistive stage must not inert the task canvas.
references.rejected: Did not implement the stage as a modal dialog.

references.name: Playwright ARIA snapshots
references.borrowed: Added ARIA snapshots for the summary stub and promoted stage states.
references.rejected: Did not rely on screenshots alone for semantic coverage.

references.name: Playwright visual comparisons
references.borrowed: Added deterministic visual comparisons for summary, promoted, folded, and downgraded states.
references.rejected: Did not snapshot volatile whole-page areas when a bounded region was enough.

references.name: Playwright emulation
references.borrowed: Used viewport and reduced-motion emulation for desktop and narrow folded fixtures.
references.rejected: Did not create a separate mobile workflow.

references.name: Playwright browser contexts
references.borrowed: Kept 424 tests isolated with separate browser contexts and traces.
references.rejected: Did not share browser state across fixtures.

references.name: Next.js layouts and pages
references.borrowed: Used persistent-layout thinking: the assistive stage is slotted into the existing shell instead of replacing it.
references.rejected: Did not add new route segments because this repo's workspace route family is Vite-driven.

references.name: Linear Inbox
references.borrowed: Borrowed low-noise notification and filtering posture for summary-first assistive awareness.
references.rejected: Did not make assistive support a global notification center.

references.name: Linear display options
references.borrowed: Kept secondary-region controls explicit, local, and reversible.
references.rejected: Did not expose wide customization that could bypass trust law.

references.name: Carbon UI shell right panel
references.borrowed: Borrowed a consistent right-side panel width and persistent shell relationship.
references.rejected: Did not float the stage over page content because the case canvas must stay dominant.

references.name: Carbon contained list
references.borrowed: Used compact contained rows for dense provenance, queue, and coexistence summaries.
references.rejected: Did not use multi-column tables inside the stage.

references.name: Carbon data table
references.borrowed: Used the warning against cramped tables to keep the stage rows compact and scannable.
references.rejected: Did not place data tables inside the side stage.

references.name: Carbon tabs
references.borrowed: Used tabs only for same-context folded support, not navigation.
references.rejected: Did not overload the narrow tab panel with multiple unrelated categories.
